title: Smoky Thai Gratin Chicken
description: A twist on traditional Thai flavors, this smoky chicken gratin dish is sure to become a favorite. With the crunch of casabe and the tang of coconut milk, this meal is a true delight.
instructions: Season the chicken with salt, pepper, smoky paprika, and brown sugar.
In a large skillet, heat a couple tablespoons of oil over medium-high heat.
Add the chicken to the skillet and sear until browned on both sides.
Transfer the chicken to a baking dish and pour in the coconut milk.
Sprinkle the grated cheese and crushed casabe over the top of the chicken.
Bake in the preheated oven for 25-30 minutes, or until the cheese is melted and bubbly.
Remove from the oven and garnish with chopped cilantro.
Serve hot, with steamed vegetables or rice if desired.
Leftovers can be refrigerated for up to 3 days or frozen for up to 2 months.
